_sources: Official DePuy Spring Creek hand-drawn map (Rod Walinchus), provided by Luyen 2026-07-15
Photo EXIF + debrief transcripts for trip-specific anchors
_orientation: Creek flows roughly south-to-north, parallel to US-89, joining the Yellowstone. 'Upper' = south/upstream (Armstrong border), 'Lower' = north/downstream (Yellowstone confluence). On the hand-drawn map north is LEFT.
owners_note: Betty Smith, Daryl & Theresa Smith invite you to enjoy our creek (per map)

sections.name: Upper DePuy's
sections.description: Upper pond, slough, Annie's Run, Betty's Riffle, PhD Pool; runs to a southern culvert at the Armstrong Spring Creek border fence.
sections.trip_relevance: The fly shop sits here. PhD Pool is 'at the very, very top' (Thorne, session 2). Luyen's Friday photo of the closed shop = 45.5547, -110.5906.

sections.name: Middle Section
sections.description: From Dick's Pond up to the Narrows. Deep holes, undercut banks. Angler's Hut with parking.
sections.trip_relevance: Saturday's water (photo cluster 45.5611-45.5669) falls in this reach. Likely the 'picnic table place' parking with the big pool above it. Confidence: medium, pending research agent.

sections.name: Lower DePuy's
sections.description: From Dick's Pond's waterfall outlet down to the Yellowstone confluence. Oxygen-rich riffles. Eva's Hut and the Main House (white house) are at this end.
sections.trip_relevance: Sunday's water (photo cluster 45.5803-45.5826) is ~2.9 km downstream of the shop, in or near this reach. Thorne hit for the cycle here Sunday morning.

features.name: Buzz's Fly Shop
features.note: Exact from IMG_1584 EXIF (Fri 2:55 PM, shop closed). Yellowstone Angler: 'Buzz's fly shop is located next to Betty's Riffle.' This resolves the transcript's 'get flies from Buzz, but he wasn't there.'

features.name: Betty's Riffle
features.note: Adjacent to the fly shop (both sources).

features.name: PhD Pool
features.note: 'One of the best, yet most challenging holes' (Yellowstone Angler). 'At the very, very top' (Thorne). Upper section, near the southern culvert / Armstrong border. THE name behind 'PhD fish' in the anglers' vocabulary.

features.name: The Narrows
features.note: Upstream limit of the Middle Section description.

features.name: Angler's Hut
features.note: Middle Section, with parking (hand map). Warming hut with stove and firewood.

features.name: Dick's Pond
features.note: Main pond fronting the white house; mostly browns. Waterfall outlet marks the top of Lower DePuy's.

features.name: Dick's Riffle
features.note: Labeled on hand map near Main House area.

features.name: Main House
features.note: The white house fronted by Dick's Pond.

features.name: Main Entrance
features.note: Hand map, west side off the county road; 'one mile to Main Entrance' from Hwy 89 label.

features.name: Southern culvert / Armstrong border fence
features.note: Top of the property. Thorne, reviewing the photo map: 'So that is at a culvert.'

july_entomology.pmd: Blanket PMD hatches; Ephemerella infrequens #14-16 (mid-June to early July) then Ephemerella inermis #18-20 (mid-July through August) — the trip's size 18-20 rusty spinner matches E. inermis exactly.
july_entomology.note_for_flybox: Rusty spinner = the spent-wing spinner stage of the PMD. Jamie's tip about 1-in-5-to-7 naturals having a red body pairs with this.
